top_risks: IDOR in subscription update (C-01) — any authenticated user can modify any subscription
Missing admin capability checks on 4 API classes (H-04 through H-08) — privilege escalation
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints (H-02) — brute-force attacks
Stateless tokens cannot be revoked (H-03) — stolen tokens valid for 24h
Unbounded bulk subscription endpoint (M-15) — DoS via database exhaustion
Email change without confirmation (M-16) — permanent account lockout risk

strengths: Consistent use of $wpdb->prepare() for parameterized queries
Input sanitization with sanitize_text_field/sanitize_email across API endpoints
HMAC-based token signing with hash_equals for timing-safe comparison
Nonce verification in WordPress admin forms
GDPR compliance with consent tracking, data export, and right to be forgotten
ABSPATH guard on all PHP files to prevent direct access
No hardcoded API keys — all credentials loaded from Config/options
No unsafe eval(), extract(), or unserialize() usage
